name: abtop
description: Like htop, but for AI coding agents. Monitor Claude    Code & Codex CLI sessions, tokens, context window,    rate limits, and ports in real-time.
readme_summary: abtop is a terminal-based monitoring tool for AI coding agents, inspired by htop. It provides real-time insights into the performance and resource usage of agents like Claude Code, Codex CLI, and OpenCode, displaying token consumption, rate limits, context window status, and active network ports.
key_features: Real-time monitoring of AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex CLI, OpenCode)
Tracks token usage, context window percentage, and rate limits
Detects open ports and child processes spawned by agents
Supports multiple active agent sessions across macOS, Linux, and Windows
Terminal User Interface (TUI) with themes and customizable display
Optional JSON output for scripting and integration
tmux integration for session jumping
primary_use_case: Monitoring and managing the performance and resource utilization of multiple AI coding agent sessions in a terminal environment.
cover_image_prompt: A vibrant, 3D isometric illustration of a futuristic control panel. In the center, a holographic dashboard displays multiple glowing, stylized AI agent icons, each with dynamic bars representing token usage, rate limits, and context window fullness. Small, subtle digital particles flow between the agents and the central display, symbolizing data monitoring. One agent icon has a warning light, indicating a potential issue. The background is a clean, modern terminal interface with subtle code snippets. The repository name 'abtop' is subtly integrated into the panel's design. The overall mood is informative and efficient, with a cool blue and green color palette.

name: docker-android
description: 🤖 A minimal and customizable Docker image running the Android emulator as a service.
readme_summary: This repository provides a minimal and customizable Docker image for running the Android emulator as a service. It's designed for headless execution, making it ideal for CI/CD pipelines and automated testing environments.
key_features: Minimal Alpine based image with Android emulator and KVM support
Bundles Java Runtime Environment 11
Customizable Android version, device type, and image types
Port-forwarding of emulator and ADB
Emulator images are wiped on restart
Headless execution suitable for CI farms
Compatible with scrcpy for remote control
primary_use_case: Running Android emulators in a Docker container for automated testing and CI/CD pipelines.
cover_image_prompt: A miniature, highly detailed Android robot character carefully assembling a complex, floating Android emulator device within a transparent, glowing Docker container. The robot is using tiny tools and blueprints, with streams of code flowing into the container as raw material. The assembled emulator displays a vibrant, simplified app interface. Subtle UI elements of a CI pipeline dashboard are visible in the background, suggesting automated workflows. The overall scene is clean and futuristic, rendered in a 3D isometric illustration style with a bright, tech-focused color palette and soft ambient lighting, emphasizing precision and automation. The repository name 'docker-android' is subtly integrated into the container's label.

name: MiroShark
description: Simulate anything, for $1 & less than 10 min - Universal Swarm Intelligence Engine
readme_summary: MiroShark is a universal swarm intelligence engine that simulates complex scenarios by spawning hundreds of AI agents. These agents interact within a simulated environment, posting, arguing, and trading based on an initial input, providing insights and reports on the simulated outcomes.
key_features: Simulate anything from press releases to policy drafts
Hundreds of grounded AI agents (Twitter, Reddit, prediction market)
Hour-by-hour simulation progression
Interactive simulation: chat with agents, drop breaking news, fork timelines
Generates reports with cited posts and trades
Quick start with OpenRouter key and launcher
Multiple installation paths (Cloud deploy, Docker, local Ollama, Claude Code CLI)
primary_use_case: Simulating and forecasting outcomes of various scenarios using swarm intelligence agents.
cover_image_prompt: A bustling, futuristic marketplace where hundreds of diverse, stylized AI avatars are actively engaged in discussions, trading digital assets, and posting on virtual bulletin boards. In the center, a single, glowing 'input' crystal (representing a press release or policy) is being observed and reacted to by all avatars. Subtle UI elements showing social media feeds and market graphs are integrated into the background. The overall mood is dynamic and analytical, with a clean, modern aesthetic. The repository name 'MiroShark' is subtly etched onto a central pillar. 3D isometric illustration style, vibrant colors, high detail.

name: devFind
description: devFind is an open source project that aims to create a platform for developers to showcase their skills and connect with potential collaborators, all in a user-friendly and searchable format.
readme_summary: devFind is an open-source platform designed for developers to create profiles showcasing their skills and connect with potential collaborators. Profiles are created in JSON format and are discoverable through a user-friendly, searchable web interface.
key_features: Search functionality for developers based on skills, location, or name
Platform for developers to showcase skills and connect with collaborators
User-friendly and searchable format for discovering developers
Developer profiles created in JSON format
primary_use_case: To serve as a discoverable directory for developers to showcase their skills and find collaborators or employment opportunities.
cover_image_prompt: A vibrant, stylized map of a digital city where each building represents a developer's profile. A friendly guide, holding a glowing magnifying glass, points towards a specific building, highlighting its unique skill set (e.g., a gear for engineering, a paintbrush for design). Small, glowing data streams connect different buildings, symbolizing collaboration. In the background, a subtle UI overlay shows search filters and results. The overall mood is welcoming and interconnected, rendered in a clean, 3D isometric illustration style with bright, optimistic colors, emphasizing discovery and connection within the developer community. The repository name 'devFind' is subtly integrated into a prominent landmark.

name: react-datepicker
description: A simple and reusable datepicker component for React
readme_summary: React Date Picker is a versatile and reusable date selection component for React applications. It offers extensive customization options, including time selection, localization, and custom header support, making it easy to integrate date and time picking functionality into your UI.
key_features: Reusable Datepicker component for React
Supports date and time selection
Localization support
Custom header configuration
Integration with date-fns and moment.js
Configurable time intervals
Click outside handler
primary_use_case: To provide developers with a flexible and easy-to-use date and time selection component within React applications.
cover_image_prompt: A friendly, anthropomorphic calendar with a smiling face, holding a magnifying glass over a specific date on its own body. Around the calendar, digital particles representing selected dates float upwards, transforming into small, elegant clock faces. In the background, subtle UI elements resembling a React component interface are visible, with the 'React Date Picker' logo subtly integrated into a digital display. The scene is set in a clean, bright digital space with a soft, inviting glow. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with clear, friendly shapes and a vibrant, accessible color palette.
